name: ACMP
description: CALM architecture for the Authentication Credential Management Platform based on the current requirements baseline, FRS, and technical specification.

nodes.name: Authentication Credential Management Platform
nodes.description: Central internal platform for service and client registration, administrative identity and role management, credential lifecycle management, HMAC issuance, authorization metadata, and audit governance.

nodes.name: AccessViewer
nodes.description: Read-only internal user who can view service or client access listings and credential metadata.

nodes.name: AccessOperator
nodes.description: Internal operator who can issue, rotate, revoke, disable, and package credentials with standard grace periods.

nodes.name: AccessAdministrator
nodes.description: Elevated internal administrator who can perform operator actions, configure extended grace periods, and view audit data.

nodes.name: Admin Web Portal
nodes.description: Internal AdminLTE-based web portal used to administer services, credentials, and platform configuration.

nodes.name: Credential Management API
nodes.description: Management API that supports administrative user management, client registration, credential issuance, rotation, revocation, package issuance, and audit-related operations.

nodes.name: Embedded Identity Provider
nodes.description: Application-local identity component that authenticates administrative users from persisted records and issues bearer tokens for management API access.

nodes.name: Protected Recipient Service
nodes.description: Internal protected service or business API that validates inbound HMAC requests and enforces credential scope and permission checks.

nodes.name: Client Service
nodes.description: Internal client service that signs outbound HMAC requests when calling protected services.

nodes.name: MiniKMS
nodes.description: Internal cryptographic service used for HMAC secret generation, envelope encryption, decryption, persisted key lifecycle management, and MiniKMS audit capture.

nodes.name: MiniKMS State Persistence Store
nodes.description: MiniKMS persistence layer that may run in file-backed, SQL Server, PostgreSQL, or demo in-memory mode depending on deployment configuration.

nodes.name: MiniKMS Key Store
nodes.description: Logical data store for MiniKMS master-key versions, active-key tracking, and retired-key lifecycle state.

nodes.name: MiniKMS Audit Store
nodes.description: Logical data store for MiniKMS operational audit events such as key creation, activation, retirement, and secret-operation activity.

nodes.name: Credential Persistence Store
nodes.description: SQL Server, PostgreSQL, or demo in-memory persistence layer that contains the platform's logical data stores.

nodes.name: Credential Record Store
nodes.description: Logical data store for service or client records, credential metadata, scopes, and HMAC detail records.

nodes.name: Recipient Protection Binding Store
nodes.description: Logical data store for recipient protection bindings such as X.509 references and registered external RSA public-key bindings used for package issuance.

nodes.name: Administrative Identity Store
nodes.description: Logical data store for persisted admin users, password material, login metadata, and admin role assignments.

nodes.name: Audit Event Store
nodes.description: Logical data store for administrative and security audit events.

nodes.name: Encrypted Credential Package
nodes.description: Versioned encrypted JSON package artifact issued per KeyId for recipient-side HMAC validation in EncryptedFile mode, bound to an approved protection context such as X.509 or a registered external RSA public-key binding.

nodes.name: Encrypted Client Credential Package
nodes.description: Versioned encrypted JSON package artifact issued per KeyId for client-side outbound HMAC signing, bound to an approved protection context such as X.509 or a registered external RSA public-key binding.

flows.name: Authenticate Administrative User
flows.description: An access user signs in through the admin portal, the embedded identity provider validates persisted credentials and role assignments, and a bearer token is issued for later management API calls.

transitions.description: An access user opens the sign-in experience in the admin portal.
Admin portal submits local username and password credentials to the embedded identity provider.
Embedded identity provider loads persisted admin-user records, password hashes, and role assignments and updates login metadata.

flows.name: Manage Administrative Users
flows.description: AccessAdministrator creates, updates, disables, or re-roles persisted administrative users through the admin portal and management API.

transitions.description: AccessAdministrator opens administrative user management in the admin portal.
Admin portal submits create, update, disable, password-reset, or role-assignment requests to the credential management API.
Credential management API persists administrative user records, password material, and role assignments.
Credential management API records the administrative user-management audit event.

flows.name: Reset Administrative User Password
flows.description: AccessAdministrator resets a persisted administrative user's password through the admin portal and management API, with audit capture.

transitions.description: AccessAdministrator opens the password reset action for an administrative user in the admin portal.
Admin portal submits the password reset request to the credential management API.
Credential management API updates the persisted password hash, salt, and related user metadata.
Credential management API records the administrative password reset audit event.

flows.name: Assign Administrative User Role
flows.description: AccessAdministrator assigns or changes persisted administrative-user role memberships through the admin portal and management API, with audit capture.

transitions.description: AccessAdministrator opens role assignment management for an administrative user in the admin portal.
Admin portal submits the role assignment change request to the credential management API.
Credential management API persists the updated administrative-user role assignments.
Credential management API records the administrative role assignment audit event.

flows.name: Register Client Service
flows.description: AccessAdministrator creates a new internal client or service record through the portal and management API.

transitions.description: AccessAdministrator starts client or service registration from the admin portal.
Admin portal submits the create client request to the credential management API.
Credential management API stores the new client or service record.

flows.name: Update Client Service
flows.description: AccessAdministrator updates client or service metadata through the portal and management API.

transitions.description: AccessAdministrator opens the client or service update action in the admin portal.
Admin portal submits the metadata update request to the credential management API.
Credential management API persists the updated client or service metadata.

flows.name: Disable Client Service
flows.description: AccessAdministrator disables a client or service through the portal and management API.

transitions.description: AccessAdministrator starts the disable action for the client or service from the admin portal.
Admin portal submits the disable request to the credential management API.
Credential management API updates persisted client or service status to disabled.

flows.name: Issue HMAC Credential
flows.description: AccessOperator or AccessAdministrator issues a new HMAC credential using the portal, management API, remote MiniKMS, and persistence stores.

transitions.description: AccessOperator starts HMAC credential issuance from the admin portal.
Admin portal submits the HMAC credential issuance request to the credential management API.
Credential management API requests HMAC secret generation and protection from MiniKMS.
MiniKMS records audit evidence of the protected secret-generation operation without storing plaintext material.
Credential management API persists encrypted secret material and credential metadata.
Credential management API records the credential issuance audit event.

flows.name: Rotate HMAC Credential (Standard)
flows.description: AccessOperator or AccessAdministrator rotates an HMAC credential with immediate cutover or a standard grace period up to 14 days using the portal, management API, remote MiniKMS, and persistence stores.

transitions.description: AccessOperator starts standard HMAC credential rotation from the admin portal.
Admin portal submits the standard credential rotation request to the credential management API.
Credential management API requests new secret generation and protection from MiniKMS for the replacement credential.
MiniKMS records audit evidence of the protected secret-rotation operation.
Credential management API persists replacement credential state and grace-period metadata when applicable.
Credential management API records the rotation audit event for the standard-grace path.

flows.name: Rotate HMAC Credential (Extended Grace)
flows.description: AccessAdministrator rotates an HMAC credential with an extended grace period longer than 14 days up to 30 days, with explicit operational reason and audit traceability.

transitions.description: AccessAdministrator starts extended-grace HMAC credential rotation from the admin portal.
Admin portal submits the extended-grace credential rotation request and operational reason to the credential management API.
Credential management API requests new secret generation and protection from MiniKMS for the replacement credential.
MiniKMS records audit evidence of the protected secret-rotation operation for the extended-grace path.
Credential management API persists replacement credential state and extended grace-period metadata.
Credential management API records audit evidence of the acting role and extended-grace reason.

flows.name: Manage Recipient Protection Bindings
flows.description: AccessOperator or AccessAdministrator registers, activates, retires, or reviews recipient protection bindings such as X.509 references and externally provisioned RSA public keys.

transitions.description: AccessOperator starts recipient protection binding management from the admin portal.
Admin portal submits the recipient protection binding request to the credential management API.
Credential management API persists or reads recipient protection binding metadata and public-key material.
Credential management API records the recipient protection binding audit event.

flows.name: Rotate Recipient Protection Binding
flows.description: AccessOperator or AccessAdministrator rolls a recipient protection binding to a new public key or X.509 reference and re-issues packages against the new binding.

transitions.description: AccessOperator starts recipient protection binding rotation from the admin portal.
Admin portal submits the recipient protection binding rotation request to the credential management API.
Credential management API persists the new active recipient protection binding and retires or flags the previous binding as needed.
Credential management API can re-issue recipient-side encrypted packages against the new active binding.
Credential management API records the recipient protection binding rotation audit event.

flows.name: Manage MiniKMS Key Versions
flows.description: AccessAdministrator performs internal MiniKMS key creation, activation, or retirement operations against the MiniKMS service, with persisted key-state and audit capture.

transitions.description: AccessAdministrator submits an internal MiniKMS key-management request.
MiniKMS persists key-version records and updates active or retired lifecycle state.
MiniKMS records the key-management audit event with actor, action, outcome, and target key version.

flows.name: View MiniKMS Audit Log
flows.description: AccessAdministrator reads MiniKMS operational audit records directly from the MiniKMS service for internal cryptographic governance.

transitions.description: AccessAdministrator requests recent MiniKMS audit data.
MiniKMS reads persisted MiniKMS audit records from the MiniKMS audit store.

flows.name: Revoke HMAC Credential
flows.description: AccessOperator or AccessAdministrator revokes an HMAC credential through the portal and management API.

transitions.description: AccessOperator starts credential revocation from the admin portal.
Admin portal submits the credential revocation request to the credential management API.
Credential management API persists revoked credential state.
Credential management API records the credential revocation audit event.

flows.name: Issue Encrypted Credential Package
flows.description: AccessOperator or AccessAdministrator uses the portal and management API to issue a versioned recipient-side encrypted package envelope bound to the target protection context.

transitions.description: AccessOperator starts encrypted credential package issuance from the admin portal.
Admin portal submits the issuance request to the credential management API.
Credential management API resolves the selected recipient protection binding and its metadata.
Credential management API issues the recipient-side encrypted JSON package envelope with binding and cryptographic metadata.

flows.name: Validate Inbound HMAC Request (KmsBacked)
flows.description: Protected recipient service validates an inbound HMAC request using cache-first runtime validation with persistence and MiniKMS on cache miss.

transitions.description: Client service sends an HMAC-signed request to the protected recipient service.
Protected recipient service resolves credential metadata when runtime cache is missing or stale.
Protected recipient service decrypts protected secret material using MiniKMS when required.

flows.name: Validate Inbound HMAC Request (EncryptedFile)
flows.description: Protected recipient service validates an inbound HMAC request using an encrypted credential package file.

transitions.description: Client service sends an HMAC-signed request to the protected recipient service.
Protected recipient service loads and validates encrypted credential package contents for request validation.

flows.name: Sign Outbound HMAC Request
flows.description: Client service signs an outbound request using the client package and then sends the signed request to the protected recipient service.

transitions.description: Client service loads protected signing material from the encrypted client credential package.
Client service sends the signed request to the protected recipient service.

flows.name: Reload Recipient Credential Package
flows.description: Protected recipient service detects atomic replacement or refresh of a recipient-side package file, validates it, and reloads it securely.

transitions.description: Protected recipient service reads the replacement package envelope, validates schema, package identity, protection binding metadata, and encrypted payload, and decrypts it before activation.

flows.name: Reload Client Credential Package
flows.description: Client service detects atomic replacement or refresh of a client-side package file, validates it, and reloads it securely.

transitions.description: Client service reads the replacement package envelope, validates schema, package identity, protection binding metadata, and encrypted payload, and decrypts it before activation.

flows.name: List Credential Metadata
flows.description: AccessViewer, AccessOperator, or AccessAdministrator lists credential metadata without exposing plaintext secrets.

transitions.description: AccessViewer opens credential metadata listing in the admin portal.
Admin portal requests credential metadata from the credential management API.
Credential management API reads credential metadata from the credential record store.

flows.name: Issue Encrypted Client Credential Package
flows.description: AccessOperator or AccessAdministrator uses the portal and management API to issue a versioned client-side encrypted package envelope bound to the target protection context.

transitions.description: AccessOperator starts encrypted client credential package issuance from the admin portal.
Admin portal submits the client package issuance request to the credential management API.
Credential management API resolves the selected recipient protection binding and its metadata.
Credential management API issues the client-side encrypted JSON package envelope with binding and cryptographic metadata.

flows.name: View Audit Log
flows.description: AccessAdministrator views audit data through the admin portal and management API.

transitions.description: AccessAdministrator opens the audit log view in the admin portal.
Admin portal requests audit data from the credential management API.
Credential management API reads audit records from the audit event store.
